abbasid_ribs.name: Abbasid Ribs
abbasid_ribs.description: Succulent Abbasid ribs, made using a time-honored Abbasidian recipe.

abbasidose.name: Abbasidose
abbasidose.description: A compound made out of red, green and blue elements.

ace_of_spades.name: Ace of Spades
ace_of_spades.description: Finely honed from old metal, the Ace of Spades is made for the heavy digger. Some say it's overkill, but anyone who dreams of spending another perfect day ruling the world of diggery with an iron fist, this is the ideal purchase, lemme tell you.

alchemical_tongs.name: Alchemical Tongs
alchemical_tongs.description: Handy bendy Alchemical Tongs help you to create exciting metals like Tin, Copper and Molybdenum by adding elements to boring old hot smelted metal.

all_spice.name: Allspice
all_spice.description: Some basic spice, freshly harvested from a Spice Plant.

alphose.name: Alphose
alphose.description: A compound made out of red, green and blue.

antique_spigot.name: Antique Spigot
antique_spigot.description: Water once flowed freely through this residual, rusting spigot. Old water, from times long by. Surely, you must have it!

apple.name: Apple
apple.description: A boldly red and brazenly juicy apple.

applejack.name: Applejack
applejack.description: A bowl of apple-y, oat-y, cinnamon-y applejack. Yummy hot or cold.

artifact_bag_of_tent_pegs.name: Bag of Tent Pegs
artifact_bag_of_tent_pegs.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Even by the standards of the Lemish of the beforetimes, these tent pegs are tiny. Discovered by Birdie and Swift when they were just two particularly inquisitive children, pegged out in a curious star-like shape on a hillside down near a far off shore, surrounded by broken string. Systematically, they unpegged each one, and brought them home to pin maps to the walls.

artifact_ball_of_string.name: Ball of String
artifact_ball_of_string.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. This ball of string - which appears to be a spun mixture of long blonde hair and patch-weeds - was discovered by Birdie and Swift when they were on one of their major childhood explorations of the North/South-Western coastline. Thinner than any string they’d ever seen, and stronger, the ball is now very depleted, much of it having been used for tough yet lightweight travel socks.

artifact_bee_key.name: U.S. Bee Key
artifact_bee_key.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The most serious part of the quinto-annual festivities of the feast of Humbaba (Humbabarians love a party) is the solemn parade circle of the bee army. Trained by bee-experts, the Unilateral Squadron of Bees (or U.S.Bee) would circle the forest clearing, each wearing one of Humbaba’s treasured fancy bee hats, each with a folded note containing a secret tucked underneath it. This key unlocked the strongroom in which these hats were kept.

artifact_bronze_ladle.name: Bronze Ladle
artifact_bronze_ladle.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift given to children born under the auspices of Grendaline, this heavy bronze ladle was used by Phoebe on the giant’s feast day to distribute a thick spicy fish-based stew - known as Grendlebo - to those attending the festivities. In line with Grendaline’s love of multi-use objects, it could also be used as a sturdy hat.

artifact_butterfly_hair_clip.name: Butterfly Bone Hair Clip
artifact_butterfly_hair_clip.description: This hair-clip belonged to Gwendolyn, but was lost when a strong wind from a quick-approaching storm swept belongings out her open window. Originally given to her by her cousin, Earnest, on the occasion of her memorization of the 137 verses of the first Book of Cosma, the clip is made of hand carved butterfly bones and encrusted with the dust of shiny elements.

artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece1.name: Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip
artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece1.description: One of four fragments that make up Gwendolyn's long-forgotten hair-clip. Acquire them all to remember it again.

artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece2.name: Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip
artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece2.description: One of four fragments that make up Gwendolyn's long-forgotten hair-clip. Acquire them all to remember it again.

artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece3.name: Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip
artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece3.description: One of four fragments that make up Gwendolyn's long-forgotten hair-clip. Acquire them all to remember it again.

artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece4.name: Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip
artifact_butterfly_hair_clip_piece4.description: One of four fragments that make up Gwendolyn's long-forgotten hair-clip. Acquire them all to remember it again.

artifact_butterfly_windchimes.name: Butterfly Windchimes
artifact_butterfly_windchimes.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Crafted by a young Egeria, decorated with ornate butterflies, made out of spice-tree leaves, dried, decorated with elemental pigments and then sealed with a varnish made from a distilled mixture of birch syrup and piggy spittle, the one thing missing from these windchimes is anything to actually make it chime. The uncouth noise was thought to ruin the mood and perfect serenity of anyone who got too close.

artifact_butterlfy_hair_clip_piece1.name: A Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip

artifact_butterlfy_hair_clip_piece2.name: A Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip

artifact_butterlfy_hair_clip_piece3.name: A Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip

artifact_butterlfy_hair_clip_piece4.name: A Piece of Butterfly Bone Hair Clip

artifact_button_bird.name: Button in the Shape of a Bird
artifact_button_bird.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. For a while, the intricately carved buttons that fastened the garments of Grendalinians were the envy of everyone. Though slightly scuffed and with a chip to the beak, this button, which once adorned the bonnet of Phoebe - a Grendalinian cocktail guru with an ever-present glint in her eye - so perfectly resembles a miniature Sandpiper that the fact it doesn’t run off onto nearby beach is a constant surprise to anyone who sees it.

artifact_button_cloud.name: Button in the Shape of a Cloud
artifact_button_cloud.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Helen, sister of Harris, spent months making this button, marked down in the annals of Grendaline as one of the most beautiful ever to fasten a pair of trousers. Using individual strands of batterfly silk, harvested from their tough underground webs, Helen slowly wound, twisted and knotted until she had a soft, delicate, but indestructible cloud-shaped button. Only a buttonbarian would say it now looks like pocket fluff.

artifact_button_frog.name: Button in the Shape of a Frog
artifact_button_frog.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Button-making - creating perfect miniatures out of moulded sudstone, carved dullite or cast ore - was once the most highly prized and closely guarded skill of those who sought Grendaline’s favor. Harris Toofanies, the Grendalinian whose cape this button once graced, made up for his unfortunate name with his exemplary button-making skill. Careful examination and cleaning reveals that the eyes on the frog button, individually carved out of sapphire, move as the wearer walks.

artifact_button_wasp.name: Button in the Shape of a Wasp
artifact_button_wasp.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Created by master-buttonwrangler Phil O’Kleon to fasten the bonnet he wore on the Feast of Grendaline, this button was renowned not only for its vivid stripes of amber and dullite, but by the fact that if the body is twisted, very carefully, and then released, it buzzed loud enough to knock spices from the trees and cause small avalanches in the wintery place from some miles away. This function was disabled some time before the great forgetting.

artifact_carved_ear.name: Intricately Carved Wooden Ear
artifact_carved_ear.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Artifactologists believe that this is one of seven ears that would have graced the mask worn by the chief dancer on the feast of Grendaline. Known for their rhythm, their ribald and raucuous moves and their apparent tirelessness, people would come from miles around to see the Grendalinian dancers. This ear would have fitted on the elaborate full-face mask that formed the most impressive - well, the only, really - part of their costume.

artifact_caterpillar_trousers.name: Caterpillar Trousers
artifact_caterpillar_trousers.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Perhaps the most useless piece of clothing in existence, this pair of trousers - pair is possibly the wrong word for something with 16 legs - this Multiplicity of Trouser was created by a loyal Humbabarian as a gift for a passing caterpillar, in order to cover his modesty. However, since caterpillars famously have no modesty (or, in fact, shame) not a single trouser in this multiplicitous garmental gathering have even been worn.

artifact_chicken_brick.name: Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick.description: Children of the houses of Friendly got used at a young age to their special skill of squeezing chickens. Partly because they were taught at night, when chickens are notoriously easier to sneak up on, and partly because, once they went to bed, they were put down with a heated brick in the shape of poultry. This is one of those.

artifact_chicken_brick_piece1.name: Piece of Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick_piece1.description: A piece of brick that resembles one fifth of a chicken and is warm to the touch. All of them will likely combine to make a whole fowl.

artifact_chicken_brick_piece2.name: Piece of Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick_piece2.description: A piece of brick that resembles one fifth of a chicken and is warm to the touch. All of them will likely combine to make a whole fowl.

artifact_chicken_brick_piece3.name: Piece of Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick_piece3.description: A piece of brick that resembles one fifth of a chicken and is warm to the touch. All of them will likely combine to make a whole fowl.

artifact_chicken_brick_piece4.name: Piece of Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick_piece4.description: A piece of brick that resembles one fifth of a chicken and is warm to the touch. All of them will likely combine to make a whole fowl.

artifact_chicken_brick_piece5.name: Piece of Chicken-Shaped Brick
artifact_chicken_brick_piece5.description: A piece of brick that resembles one fifth of a chicken and is warm to the touch. All of them will likely combine to make a whole fowl.

artifact_daybag.name: Remains of a Daybag
artifact_daybag.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Apart from a sprinkling of shiny element dust gathered in the corners, it would be easy to discount it as a particularly well structured old pigskin trash-bag, apart from the a) the sturdy handle, b) that would be a waste of a good piggy and c) no one piggy would be enough for this bag, which is large enough when closed to hold a 1200-page manuscript, or possibly an infant.

artifact_disembodied_dentures.name: Rictus Disembodied Dentures
artifact_disembodied_dentures.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Pelagea, as a child, was the first person to notice this set of hauntingly grinning teeth, perched in a tree. Though no Humbabarian could dislodge them, many theories were raised and arboreal myths concocted explaining the genesis of the jaws involving death, decay, and flaying. The idea that Humbaba might have placed them up there just to mess with people was never discussed. It should have been.

artifact_ear_trumpet.name: Ear Trumpet
artifact_ear_trumpet.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Carefully curved to hear not only from short distances, but long distances, through walls and round corners as well. This ear trumpet once belonged to a loyal supplicant of Friendly, Anton, who would use it to pick up all manner of snippets of juicy gossip and scandalous rumor, and then to relay it to the giant as he worked, turning it around to form a megaphone, and standing on Friendly’s shoulder to be heard.

artifact_ebony_teaspoon.name: Ebony Teaspoon
artifact_ebony_teaspoon.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift for any child born under the moons of Friendly, this ebony teaspoon, as dark and almost as strong as the coffee Friendly himself would brew, was generally kept in the refrigerator of most homes that had one for luck. The “luck” in this case being the fact that if you didn’t keep it somewhere brightly lit, you would lose it almost immediately, and Friendly’s reaction if he found out would make you feel very unlucky indeed.

artifact_fan.name: Fan
artifact_fan.description: Carefully made for Cecily in her declining years, by her great niece, Margaret, intended as a gift that brought the breath of Cosma to her aging great aunt’s cheek. It is made from the wings of brightly coloured butterflies, sewn on to a structure of plank splinters, and hinged with cherry stalks, At least, that is what it is meant to look like. Butterfly wings are delicate things.

artifact_glove_metal_finger.name: Woolly Glove Darned with a Metal Finger
artifact_glove_metal_finger.description: Brother and sister, Tanu and Sitha, once each had a pair of gloves. When one glove was lost, Tanu and Sitha used the three remaining gloves between them, holding hands inside the third wherever they went out. When another glove was lost, they only went out in snow one at a time. But down to one glove, they simply sat at home making sure the remaining glove would never be lost. The metal finger on this one may look harmless, but is unbreakable, the weight of an elephant and once on, refuses to leave the finger of whomsoever it has been placed upon.

artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece1.name: Piece of Woolly Glove Darned with a Metal Finger
artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece1.description: A shred of wool and a chunk of metal that look remarkably like one third of a glove.

artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece2.name: Piece of Woolly Glove Darned with a Metal Finger
artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece2.description: A shred of wool and a chunk of metal that look remarkably like one third of a glove.

artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece3.name: Piece of Woolly Glove Darned with a Metal Finger
artifact_glove_metal_finger_piece3.description: A shred of wool and a chunk of metal that look remarkably like one third of a glove.

artifact_goggles.name: Oversized Brass-Rimmed Protective Goggles
artifact_goggles.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. There’s a list somewhere of all the things from which this particular class of protective goggle can protect the wearer. Weena, one of the great scientists of the beforetimes, ran a series of tests flinging various substances at testers and making a mark on her notepad whenever someone said “ouch”, and a larger mark if they suddenly stopped saying anything at all. Erring on the side of caution, most goggle-wearers trusted them to protect against hard airs, soft liquids and lukewarm steam, and learned just to avoid Weena entirely.

artifact_gold_butter_knife.name: Gold Butter Knife
artifact_gold_butter_knife.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift for a baby born under the watchful eye of Humbaba, this gold butter knife was given to a tiny child called Douglas, who would spend hours gazing at its soft, rich colour and stroking its smooth rounded handle. If left in direct sunlight, he discovered, the elements that bind the knife together as a metal would break down, and the butterfly butter that forms its alchemistical base will start to melt.

artifact_handbag_clasp.name: Handbag Clasp
artifact_handbag_clasp.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Long fallen of the handbag of Egeria, a forgetful woman with a love of large handbags, this heavy metal handbag clasp, fashioned out of the strongest metal available to the formidable bag-wranglers of Groddle Forest. Furthermore, it is secured by the clasping of two perfect glass pyramids that produce a perfect rainbow shadow when hit by the sun.

artifact_heart_shaped_lump.name: Heart-Shaped Lump of Lead
artifact_heart_shaped_lump.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Apparently dropped in the middle of a busy street, this is a lump of heavy, dull grey metal in the shape of a heart. Or it would be in the shape of a heart, if it wasn’t torn almost all the way through, making it a deal more puzzling, and a lot less prized in the otherwise lucrative ‘ornamental lumpy metal hearts’ market. It cannot be melted, ground down or carried too long without killer backache.

artifact_ivory_coffeestick.name: Ivory Coffeestick
artifact_ivory_coffeestick.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift presented to a child born under the auspices of Mab, this Ivory Coffeestick is best kept out of the tiny hands of children for their own safety. Though this in theory belonged to a baby girl named Edmund, her parents considered a sharp shard of animal bone someone had once thought to stir their coffee with an inappropriate toy.

artifact_magical_pendant.name: Magical Pendant
artifact_magical_pendant.description: A high priestess of Grendaline once bestowed this piece of jewelry, known as the ‘Kommangerrit Neckpiece” to Helen, having observed that her low confidence was stalling her romantic life. Helen, who became the most desirable girl in Groddle, and believed it to have magical properties, though under duress, the priestess would admit that the vaguely suggestive shape and beer-scented pheromones sprinkled on it might have been more responsible.

artifact_magical_pendant_piece1.name: Piece of Magical Pendant
artifact_magical_pendant_piece1.description: One charming, chipped off chunk of a total of three romantically charged necklace pieces.

artifact_magical_pendant_piece2.name: Piece of Magical Pendant
artifact_magical_pendant_piece2.description: One charming, chipped off chunk of a total of three romantically charged necklace pieces.

artifact_magical_pendant_piece3.name: Piece of Magical Pendant
artifact_magical_pendant_piece3.description: One charming, chipped off chunk of a total of three romantically charged necklace pieces.

artifact_metal_tap.name: Metal Tap
artifact_metal_tap.description: Originally attached to a large heated bubble-tea-holder in the palace of Friendly, this evenly-pouring, generously-gauged tap carved to look like a chicken’s rear end, and with a handle the shape of a new moon, has subsequently been put into use straining salmon juice, broaching beaver dams, and, regrettably, tapping fermented watermelons at some of Humbaba’s crazier parties.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles.name: A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles.description: A loyal Cosmapolitan, Algie once gave this to his best friend in Groddle Forest, Jack, in retaliation for bestowing the nickname ‘Pond-scum’ upon him. A normal mirror, Algie hung it in such a way that he could reach in through Jack’s window at night and draw on it. Leading Jack to believe that he was sprouting unheard of amounts of ear hair overnight, every night.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece1.name: Piece of A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece1.description: One fifth of a shattered mirror. Combining all of them will undoubtedly bring good luck at the expense of whomever broke it.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece2.name: Piece of A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece2.description: One fifth of a shattered mirror. Combining all of them will undoubtedly bring good luck at the expense of whomever broke it.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece3.name: Piece of A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece3.description: One fifth of a shattered mirror. Combining all of them will undoubtedly bring good luck at the expense of whomever broke it.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece4.name: Piece of A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece4.description: One fifth of a shattered mirror. Combining all of them will undoubtedly bring good luck at the expense of whomever broke it.

artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece5.name: Piece of A Mirror with Scribbles On It
artifact_mirror_with_scribbles_piece5.description: One fifth of a shattered mirror. Combining all of them will undoubtedly bring good luck at the expense of whomever broke it.

artifact_mysterious_cube.name: Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube.description: The people who lived under the auspices of Lem were always a curious lot, never more so than when they stumbled on something the giant had left behind when off on travels. This cube was kept on display at the house of Isabella, the great explorer, whose theories on the different numbers of prongs and holes on each face were rich, varied, and all completely wrong.

artifact_mysterious_cube_piece1.name: Piece of Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube_piece1.description: One of five small fragments that, never mind the laws of geometry, combine to make one large cube. Mysterious.

artifact_mysterious_cube_piece2.name: Piece of Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube_piece2.description: One of five small fragments that, never mind the laws of geometry, combine to make one large cube. Mysterious.

artifact_mysterious_cube_piece3.name: Piece of Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube_piece3.description: One of five small fragments that, never mind the laws of geometry, combine to make one large cube. Mysterious.

artifact_mysterious_cube_piece4.name: Piece of Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube_piece4.description: One of five small fragments that, never mind the laws of geometry, combine to make one large cube. Mysterious.

artifact_mysterious_cube_piece5.name: Piece of Large Mysterious Cube
artifact_mysterious_cube_piece5.description: One of five small fragments that, never mind the laws of geometry, combine to make one large cube. Mysterious.

artifact_necklace_amazonite.name: Nyanite Necklace
artifact_necklace_amazonite.description: A necklace of 17 pleasingly square beads, given by devotees of Mab to celebrate Croppaday. Based on a necklace owned by an ancient Mabbite priest called Timbernasleigh which would, when placed around the right neck, play the most alluring yet annoying tune Ur had ever heard so repetitiously that anyone within earshot could never unhear it. This replica, thank Mab, does not do that.

artifact_necklace_amazonite_piece.name: Bead of Nyanite Necklace
artifact_necklace_amazonite_piece.description: A single deep-green bead, warm to the touch, from a Nyanite necklace. Aquire 17 of them to form the remembrance of a whole necklace.

artifact_necklace_imperial.name: Ouzian Necklace
artifact_necklace_imperial.description: A necklace of 23 swirling blue beads, traditionally given to family (and those loved like family) to celebrate the feast of the Sprinkling. Based on the necklace of ancient Mistress of the Water-filled Can, Nephelokokkygia (though not named after her, because during Sprinkling most people tended to lose the ability to pronounce her name).

artifact_necklace_imperial_piece.name: Bead of Ouzian Necklace
artifact_necklace_imperial_piece.description: A single swirling blue bead from a Ouzian Necklace, which, when reunited with 22 others of its kind, form the remembrance of a single flowing neck-river.

artifact_necklace_onyx.name: Gnipperite Gnecklace
artifact_necklace_onyx.description: A necklace of 19 beads, as black as night. Given by the followers of Friendly to their loved ones on Recurse Eve, in mourning for the year that had passed and holding the promise of Recurse and the year to come. It was also said that the original Gnipperite Gnecklace, owned by a ancient called Masha, had hollow beads, each big enough to hold a stiff swig of hooch. But this reproduction, cheaper version, is boringly solid.

artifact_necklace_onyx_piece.name: Bead of Gnipperite Gnecklace
artifact_necklace_onyx_piece.description: A single inky bead from a Gnipperite Gnecklace, cold, smooth, and with a faint sparkle of a billion stars when looked at closely. Acquire 19 of them to remember the whole necklace.

artifact_necklace_redtigereye.name: Red Tiger Eye Necklace
artifact_necklace_redtigereye.description: A necklace of 19 rich red beads, given one year during the annual festival of Alphcon to all who attended the festival or bought anything at it. No one knows why the name is given. There have never been Red Tigers in Ur, or, for that matter, Tigers with Red Eyes. So not only were no tigers harmed in the making of this necklace, no tigers existed to be harmed in the first place. Also: what are tigers?

artifact_necklace_redtigereye_piece.name: Bead of Red Tiger Eye Necklace
artifact_necklace_redtigereye_piece.description: A single blood-coloured bead named after the eye of a red tiger, or the red eye of a normal tiger (no one can decide) that constitutes 1/19th of a necklace.

artifact_necklace_rhyolite.name: Caiyotite Necklace
artifact_necklace_rhyolite.description: A necklace of 17 multi-coloured beads, traditionally given as gifts between the ancients to celebrate a successful Feat. Possibly the most democratically forged (and cheap) ancient jewellery, the beads were first forged when devotees of all the giants came together, each bringing their sacred stones and melding them over intense heat to form this marbled, chaotic but undeniably pleasing piece.

artifact_necklace_rhyolite_piece.name: Bead of Caiyotite Necklace
artifact_necklace_rhyolite_piece.description: A single multicoloured bead from a Caiyotite Necklace, which hums gently in the palm of the hand. 1/17th of a necklace unremarkable in beauty, low in price, but rich in sentimental value.

artifact_nose_of_china.name: Fake Nose Made of China
artifact_nose_of_china.description: Initially worn on high days and holidays by the loyal supplicants to Humbaba, this fake nose was created in the mould (literally) of Dorothy the Mad Humbabarian Elder’s original falsenez. Painted with inks made by mixing the dyes of various flowers, leaves and spices with donated piggy spittle, the faded scene on this chipped but magnificent nose is intended to portray the birth of Humbaba herself. It is a somewhat messy scene.

artifact_nose_of_china_piece1.name: Piece of Fake Nose Made of China
artifact_nose_of_china_piece1.description: A single and somewhat snotty half of a fake nose made from delicate china.

artifact_nose_of_china_piece2.name: Piece of Fake Nose Made of China
artifact_nose_of_china_piece2.description: A single and somewhat snotty half of a fake nose made from delicate china.

artifact_nose_of_plaster.name: Fake Nose Made of Plaster
artifact_nose_of_plaster.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Dorothy, who made this nose, had over the course of her life developed a firmly held belief that one day she would sneeze so hard her nose would fall off and prove itself difficult to reattach. Ever practical (if slightly delusional) Dorothy chose to prepare for her impending noselessness by creating fake noses, beautifully ornate and lightweight, to cover the problem. Though Dorothy never mislaid her nose, the fancy falsenez became extremely fashionable.

artifact_nose_of_plastic.name: Fake Nose Made of Plastic with Glasses Attached
artifact_nose_of_plastic.description: For those who belonged to the Humbabarian fashionable set, wearing a simple fake nose to a party was no longer considered a costume, since that’s what they wore all the time anyway. Pelagea, niece of Douglas and Dorothy, created a new strain of ultrafalsenez, which would attach other facial characteristics such as, in this case, a mustache of piggy eyelashes and a pair of glasses made from batterfly claws.

artifact_nose_of_wool.name: Fake Nose Made of Wool
artifact_nose_of_wool.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Once the fashion of the falsenez - started by Mad Humbabarian Elder Dorothy - had firmly caught the imagination of the Groddleforestians, the fake noses sported for the remainder of that century ranged from the practical to the frankly ridiculous. This was one of the former, a child-sized tightly-knitted woollen nose intended for cold days and misty mornings that belonged to a little boy named Adam.

artifact_paper_bag.name: Discrete Paper Bag
artifact_paper_bag.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Made out of a special paper, bound into a small pouch and made waterproof with the application of an aged mixture piggy-grease and nutmeg oil. This small crumpled bag was the constant traveling companion of Jonathan. Born into the Lemish people he, unfortunately, turned out to be a very poor traveller indeed, and would feel his stomach turn when going more than 5mph on anything but a flat surface. Artifactologists have identified this bag, but none handle it very long.

artifact_plastic_spoon.name: Plastic Spoon
artifact_plastic_spoon.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift given to any child born within the supportive, treasure-filled, but quite often vacant house of Lem (no giant loved to explore more), this plastic spoon was given to a very small baby named Jonathan. Formed of a complex combination of elements, it is good for everything except eating: a combination of heat and organic material will cause it to burst into flames.

artifact_platinumium_spork.name: Platinumium Spork
artifact_platinumium_spork.description: The traditional gift given to a child born under the auspices of Alph, this particular platinumium Spork belonged to a baby named Weena, who was only allowed to gaze at it, glinting, rounded and pointy, from her crib. Not so much because of the rarity of the material - but because of her mother Jules' fear that she would take someone’s eye out experimenting with it.

artifact_platinumium_spork_piece1.name: Piece of Platinumium Spork
artifact_platinumium_spork_piece1.description: Half of a platinuminium spork that requires its partner before functioning like a spoon, fork, or both.

artifact_platinumium_spork_piece2.name: Piece of Platinumium Spork
artifact_platinumium_spork_piece2.description: Half of a platinuminium spork that requires its partner before functioning like a spoon, fork, or both.

artifact_roll_of_blueprints.name: Roll of Blueprints for a Prototype Something
artifact_roll_of_blueprints.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. In a wild flurry of imagination, Weena the visionary had drawn, in a flash of inspiration a meticulous set of plans for a vehicle that would be able to plunge to unimaginable depths. Sketching quickly and with great urgency, there are parts where the dullite nib has almost town through the special graded scientific paper, dyed with whortleberries. Some lines and measurements are still visible though, and the words, ‘core’, ‘degrees’, ‘important’ and ‘potato’.

artifact_scarf.name: Extremely Long Scarf
artifact_scarf.description: Tanu and Sitha’s mother, a worrity woman of many arms and several dozen busy fingers, could whip up a knitted scarf like this one in twelve minutes if the wind turned. The “double rainbow” scarf (named for its colours and ability to stretch twice around the house) could bundle every child in a regular-sized Alphibian family at once. Once, a scarf was given to a tall handsome stranger who wore it all on his own, but no one caught the strange man’s name.

artifact_scrap_of_head_polishing_cloth.name: Scrap of Head Polishing Cloth
artifact_scrap_of_head_polishing_cloth.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Seeking ways to please their particular, peculiar, pristinely manicured protector, the Tii-ites were always seeking the greatest possible way of pleasing the giant with the perfectly smooth bald head. Dedicated supplicant Threepwood declared his decision to create a giant-sized head-polishing cloth. However, since it took him 47 years to collect enough batterfly silk for this small (perfect) corner, the great supplication never came to pass.

artifact_see_through_bottle.name: See-Through 2 oz Bottle
artifact_see_through_bottle.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. When Gulliver decided he needed something to steel his nerves before the cheaper domestic dragon-flights of the beforetimes, he created a new type of carry-on bottle to put it in. Made by rubbing waxy egg plant leaves until seethrough, sewing with chicken-leather and attaching a beak for a lid, it was a breakthrough in lightweight emergency booze carriage.

artifact_seed_pouch.name: Pouch Full of Old Seeds
artifact_seed_pouch.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. It is several millennia since these seeds were considered viable for producing a fruit tree crop, and some centuries since they could be considered viable for grinding into fruit-salts. However, in their solid, rollable form, they were considered very viable for games of marbles between Harris and his best friend Phil, and this, evidenced by the chips on the shells of some of the seeds, is how they were most recently utilized.

artifact_silver_spoon.name: Silver Spoon
artifact_silver_spoon.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift of any child born under the all-seeing eye of Cosma, this silver spoon belonged to a baby named Algernon. Though it seems unusual for a baby-spoon to be the size of a baby, and polished to quite this blinding shine, when the fact this gift is intended to provide the greatest view of the reflected sky from a crib - and the occasional wink of Cosma - everything makes sense.

artifact_small_water_glass.name: Small Water Glass
artifact_small_water_glass.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The followers of Friendly, if pressed, would always claim that the small water glasses you used to find in their home were for drinking strong clear spirits, or tea. But, carved with a monogram and worn down on the rim, every other resident of Groddle knew that the glasses were used to gather amusing and embarrassing tales of other giants, which supplicants could offer up in exchange for favors.

artifact_sock_garters.name: Tangled Pair of Sock Garters
artifact_sock_garters.description: No giant in the history of giants prized sock-garters like Tii. To be fair, none of the other giants (or any other living soul) could work out or knew what they were for. Nevertheless, at one point, all born under the auspice of the mighty Tii were expected to have sock garters with them at all times. Like this tangled example, owned by Elaine Melange, they were barely ever removed from a pocket, never worn, and lost in the woods before too long.

artifact_sock_hook.name: Sock Hook
artifact_sock_hook.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Masha, the daughter of Anton and greatest scholar of the laws of Friendly in the forest, used to claim that her superlative powers of memory were attributable to her diet of nothing but eggs, using a cedar plank for a flannel, and never forgetting to place her worn black socks on the special hooked sock-holder she had built herself. She was also a bit mad.

artifact_stainless_precious_metal_fork.name: Stainless Precious Metal Fork
artifact_stainless_precious_metal_fork.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. The traditional gift given to children born under the auspices of the detached but watchful giant Tii, this once pristine and perfectly balanced stainless metalwork was once given to a particularly attractive large-headed baby by the name of Marley LeChuck. Never intended as an implement of feeding (which is lucky, given the murderously pointed prongs) the Tii-ite fork was chimed against hard surfaces to produce a sonorous, resonant, meditative ’tiiiiiiiing’.

artifact_three_cornered_hat.name: Three-Cornered Hat
artifact_three_cornered_hat.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Knowing of Tii’s love of all things faceted and precise, the Tii-ites once sought to curry favor from their giant by creating hats of perfectly measured geometric shapes. The idea was that, looking down at them, Tii would merely see perfect prime-number-sided objects scuttling far below, and be pleased. The great head-measurer and millinery-material manipulator, Elaine Melange created this hat for the great Tii-ite scholar, Luigi.

artifact_toenail_clippers.name: Ornate Toenail Clippers
artifact_toenail_clippers.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Cast in the strong but malleable metals of the ore found in Lower Groddle, these fancy clippers are decorated with a misty disc of opal in the stem. At least you hope it’s opal. It’s entirely possible they’re known as ‘toenail clippers’ because this is a fragment of giant toenail, discarded, carved into a thousand thousand splinters, polished up and sold to the followers of Friendly for a scandalous number of currants. Wouldn’t be the first time.

artifact_top_hat.name: Very Tall Top Hat
artifact_top_hat.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. “Keep it under your hat” is a phrase that, for too long, has been considered a metaphor, meaning to keep something concealed, tucked into one’s most prized mind-pocket. But for the followers of Alph, this was a literal instruction. This hat, belonging to Dovim, a scientist of Alph’s highest order, was consequently one of the highest hats allowed in Alphibian society. Things that Dovim could keep under it included bottles of whiskey, blueprints, bunting and, on one occasion, his youngest child.

artifact_torn_corner_map.name: Torn Corner of a Map
artifact_torn_corner_map.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. A mess of mountain peaks appear at the very edge of this torn piece of paper look like they could be Groddle Forest heights, but the sheer number of them, and the ancient runic script by the side of them, has convinced expert artifactologists that they are, in fact, a very distant mountain range. Isabella, the great Lemish explorer roamed with this map in her Lemish knapsack for her whole life, and never even reached the foothills.

artifact_torn_manuscript.name: Torn Manuscript
artifact_torn_manuscript.description: This small fragment, dusted with shiny elements is all that remains of a VERY long very bad novel. But since, once upon a time, a tired finger took the pains to carefully hammer it out on a rudimentary typewriter, it should be kept. However, since the fragment of complex story that can be seen seems to focus on hot, passionate clinches between the giants - it’s probably best we can’t read it.

artifact_torn_manuscript_piece1.name: Piece of Torn Manuscript
artifact_torn_manuscript_piece1.description: One of four pieces of paper and frame that, when stitched together, create a string of words which probably shouldn't be read.

artifact_torn_manuscript_piece2.name: Piece of Torn Manuscript
artifact_torn_manuscript_piece2.description: One of four pieces of paper and frame that, when stitched together, create a string of words which probably shouldn't be read.

artifact_torn_manuscript_piece3.name: Piece of Torn Manuscript
artifact_torn_manuscript_piece3.description: One of four pieces of paper and frame that, when stitched together, create a string of words which probably shouldn't be read.

artifact_torn_manuscript_piece4.name: Piece of Torn Manuscript
artifact_torn_manuscript_piece4.description: One of four pieces of paper and frame that, when stitched together, create a string of words which probably shouldn't be read.

artifact_twenty_nine_cornered_hat.name: Twenty-Nine Cornered Hat
artifact_twenty_nine_cornered_hat.description: This is a lost artifact, known only through ancient stories of long-deceased ancestors, and has not been discovered in Ur. Deep in the beforetimes, the supplicants of Tii decided that in order to please their great overseer, they would make perfectly-measured, prime-number-sided hats. Elaine Melange, millinery-material manipulator had such success with the simple three, five and seven-cornered hats that she became ambitious. This unwieldy, unattractive, much battered, stained, and long-buried example of one of her “High Prime” collection is testament to her ambition’s collapse.

artifact_wooden_apple.name: Wooden Apple
artifact_wooden_apple.description: Carved of hardest spice tree wood, once adorning the mantlepiece of Gaia, mother of Helen and Harris, this apple was once painted a startlingly shiny gold. This was made by mixing a secret combination of elements, grinding them down with powdered chunks of sparkly and turning them to liquid paint with the careful addition of happy butterfly tears. So desperate were others for the secret recipe that the Apple was stolen almost daily, a little of the patina scraped off each time.

artifact_wooden_apple_piece1.name: Piece of Wooden Apple
artifact_wooden_apple_piece1.description: A splinter of spice tree wood that makes up one fourth of a most wonderful apple decoration, fit for a mantelpiece.

artifact_wooden_apple_piece2.name: Piece of Wooden Apple
artifact_wooden_apple_piece2.description: A splinter of spice tree wood that makes up one fourth of a most wonderful apple decoration, fit for a mantelpiece.

artifact_wooden_apple_piece3.name: Piece of Wooden Apple
artifact_wooden_apple_piece3.description: A splinter of spice tree wood that makes up one fourth of a most wonderful apple decoration, fit for a mantelpiece.

artifact_wooden_apple_piece4.name: Piece of Wooden Apple
artifact_wooden_apple_piece4.description: A splinter of spice tree wood that makes up one fourth of a most wonderful apple decoration, fit for a mantelpiece.

awesome_pot.name: Awesome Pot
awesome_pot.description: An awesome pot is used to make special dishes. Very special.

awesome_stew.name: Awesome Stew
awesome_stew.description: Chunky awesome stew, so thick you can eat it with a spork.

bacon.name: Rasher of Bacon
bacon.description: A delicious, greasy, surprise Piggy treat from the Piggy's newly discovered Orthogonal Meat Generator (OMG).

bag_alchemistry_kit.name: Alchemistry Kit
bag_alchemistry_kit.description: An alchemistry kit, lovingly handcrafted using wood harvested from ancient bean trees in Groddle Forest. Use it to keep your compounds organized and unexplode-y.

bag_bigger.name: Bigger Bag
bag_bigger.description: A big, big bag for putting things in. Bigger than 15 slots, but not as big as 17.

bag_bigger_blue.name: Bigger Blue Bag
bag_bigger_blue.description: A big, big blue bag for putting things in. Bigger than 15 slots, but not as big as 17.

bag_bigger_gray.name: Bigger Gray Bag
bag_bigger_gray.description: A big, big gray bag for putting things in. Bigger than 15 slots, but not as big as 17.

bag_bigger_green.name: Bigger Green Bag
bag_bigger_green.description: A big, big green bag for putting things in. Bigger than 15 slots, but not as big as 17.

bag_bigger_pink.name: Bigger Pink Bag
bag_bigger_pink.description: A big, big pink bag for putting things in. Bigger than 15 slots, but not as big as 17.

bag_butler.name: Butler
bag_butler.description: Sewn from scraps of imagination and brought to life with a breath of happy servitude, your personally customized Butler or Butlerina enjoys buttling on your Home Street and can help you with many things. These include: mail, taking messages and packages, handing out gifts, and chatting with you.

bag_cabinet_barrel.name: Barrel
bag_cabinet_barrel.description: Firm, rounded and sturd… Ah, what's the point? It's a Barrel. A big, bog-standard barrelly barrel-style barrel. It's just a Barrel. You know, like the kind of thing Hooch goes in? It's a Barrel. Move on. Nothing to see here.

bag_cubimal_case.name: Cubimal Case
bag_cubimal_case.description: For keeping your Cubimals in peak condition.

bag_elemental_pouch.name: Elemental Pouch
bag_elemental_pouch.description: An essential elemental pouch. You need it for carrying alchemical elements. What, you thought you could carry them in your HANDS? Requires Element Handling skill.

bag_furniture_cabinet.name: Cabinet
bag_furniture_cabinet.description: The kind of unassuming cabinet that looks so plain and basic you suspect it may contain a portal to a secret world, rather than shelves. It contains shelves.

bag_furniture_sdb.name: Storage Display Box
bag_furniture_sdb.description: For the pragmatic showoff in everyone, a box with hidden depths. Not only can you display your prized possessions, but you can tuck a virtually infinite amount of the exact same prized possession behind it, for safekeeping.

bag_furniture_smallcabinet.name: Small Cabinet
bag_furniture_smallcabinet.description: Rumour has it this cabinet, perceived as 'small' from the outside, is actually cavernous inside, and potentially capable of time travel. The rumours are false.

bag_furniture_wallcabinet.name: Wall Cabinet
bag_furniture_wallcabinet.description: The good thing about this cabinet is that it's at eye-level. The bad thing about it is that it's eye-level and mildly unattractive. Still, you don't need to compliment the cabinetry while you're stashing your stockpile, as they say.

bag_generic.name: Generic Bag
bag_generic.description: An honest, unpretentious burlap sack. You could probably put things in it, perhaps 10 things, and give it a name. Just a thought.

bag_generic_blue.name: Generic Blue Bag
bag_generic_blue.description: An honest, unpretentious blue burlap sack. You could probably put things in it, perhaps 10 things, and give it a name. Just a thought.

bag_generic_gray.name: Generic Gray Bag
bag_generic_gray.description: An honest, unpretentious gray burlap sack. You could probably put things in it, perhaps 10 things, and give it a name. Just a thought.

bag_generic_green.name: Generic Green Bag
bag_generic_green.description: An honest, unpretentious green burlap sack. You could probably put things in it, perhaps 10 things, and give it a name. Just a thought.

bag_generic_pink.name: Generic Pink Bag
bag_generic_pink.description: An honest, unpretentious pink burlap sack. You could probably put things in it, perhaps 10 things, and give it a name. Just a thought.

bag_gift_box.name: Wrappable Gift Box
bag_gift_box.description: An empty box, filled with promise. Not really, it's filled with nothing, but once someone (the gifter) fills it with something (the gift), wraps it, and gives it to another (the giftee) it will be filled with joy. Each box can take three stacks of joy (or three stacks of anything, in fact).

bag_gift_box_wrapped.name: Gift Box
bag_gift_box_wrapped.description: One mystery surprise gift box, filled with… well, filled with gifts. But what gifts? There's only one way to find out… No, not asking the person who gave it to you. The OTHER, non-spoilerific way. Open it!

bag_greeter_badge.name: Greeter Badge
bag_greeter_badge.description: Your genuine fondness of Glitchkind makes you uniquely qualified to greet new players as they enter the world of Glitch. And, this badge proves it. You are a unique snowflake!

bag_moving_box.name: Moving Box
bag_moving_box.description: The Glitch Advanced Moving Service (GAMS) will pack up your belongings and transfer them to your new home for you.

bag_notice_board.name: Notice Board
bag_notice_board.description: A notice board. You can post notes on it yourself, or read notes someone else has posted. Take a gander at it and see if you can find anything interesting — reading builds character!

bag_spicerack.name: Spice Rack
bag_spicerack.description: A practical rack for storing spices.

bag_toolbox_chrome.name: Chrome Toolbox
bag_toolbox_chrome.description: A sturdy, metal-y, chrome toolbox. It's a box. And you can put tools in it.

bag_toolbox_green.name: Green Toolbox
bag_toolbox_green.description: A sturdy, metal-y, green toolbox. It's a box. And you can put tools in it.

bag_toolbox_red.name: Red Toolbox
bag_toolbox_red.description: A sturdy, metal-y, red toolbox. It's a box. And you can put tools in it.

banana_no_names.name: Banana No-Names
banana_no_names.description: One banana fried to crispy, buttery perfection and drizzled with honey. Mmmm.

barnacle.name: Mortar Barnacle
barnacle.description: If clinging to things is a skill, this little fella was a champion. Once prised from a cluster (or "Blister") of pals, Barnacles can be introduced to the Grinder to find a new purpose.

barnacle_talc.name: Barnacle Talc
barnacle_talc.description: The post-Grinder form of the lovable barnacle, this smooth off-white powder is bound to be good for something. If only you knew what…

batterfly_bar.name: Batterfly Bounty Booster Bar
batterfly_bar.description: An extremely potent concoction which is rumored to produce a prodigious amount of guano from a Batterfly.

beaker.name: Beaker
beaker.description: A state-of-the-art beaker, integral for converting compounds into powders. Now available in exciting Pyrex, specifically designed for today's modern clumsy alchemist.

beam.name: Beam
beam.description: One sturdy construction beam, similar in heft and girth to a mighty Wood Tree. But then, this Beam was once some Posts; Posts made from Boards, which in turn were once a whole shedload of Planks, each hand-culled from a tender young Wood Tree. Good thing those Wood Trees didn't die in vain, eh?

bean_bean.name: Bean Tree Bean
bean_bean.description: A plain, non-magical bean tree bean. It can grow even more beans!

bean_bubble.name: Bubble Tree Bean
bean_bubble.description: A fizzy bubble tree bean. It can be planted to grow a bubble tree.

bean_egg.name: Egg Plant Bean
bean_egg.description: A smooth, round egg plant bean. It can grow egg plants. Then the fun really starts!

bean_fruit.name: Fruit Tree Bean
bean_fruit.description: An ordinary fruit tree bean. It can be used to grow a fruit tree.

bean_gas.name: Gas Plant Bean
bean_gas.description: A vapourous gas plant bean. It can be used to grow a gas plant.

bean_plain.name: Bean
bean_plain.description: A bean. It can be used for cooking, or it can be seasoned to turn it into seeds for different trees and plants.

bean_seasoner.name: Bean Seasoner
bean_seasoner.description: A Bean Seasoner seasons beans. You probably guessed that already.

bean_spice.name: Spice Plant Bean
bean_spice.description: A small spicy bean. It can be used to plant a spice plant.

bean_wood.name: Wood Tree Bean
bean_wood.description: A satisfyingly woody wood tree bean. You can use this to grow a wood tree. Guaranteed 100% splinter-free!

beer.name: Beer
beer.description: A shiny can of mmm... beer.

berry_bowl.name: Berry Bowl
berry_bowl.description: A very merry bowl of berries.

beryl.name: Chunk of Beryl
beryl.description: Halfway between sparkly and dull, it's an innocuous chunk of beryl.

best_bean_dip.name: Best Bean Dip
best_bean_dip.description: Bowl of beany, dippy bean dip.

big_salad.name: Big Salad
big_salad.description: It's bigger than small. It's a big salad.

birch_candy.name: Birch Candy
birch_candy.description: Candy made of boiled birch syrup so concentrated and so sweet that one too many and you may see god. Or at least the tooth fairy. And they'll be crying.

birch_syrup.name: Birch Syrup
birch_syrup.description: A bottle of sticky birch syrup.

black_pepper.name: Black Pepper
black_pepper.description: A mound of extra-sneezy black pepper.

blender.name: Blender
blender.description: A Blender is very good for blending juices, smoothies and other concoctions. This seems a generally sturdy model.

blockmaker.name: Blockmaker
blockmaker.description: Even simple building blocks have to come from somewhere... and this is where. Learn to harness the power of this machine and Urth Blocks of superiour blockiness shall be yours.

blockmaker_chassis.name: Blockmaker Chassis
blockmaker_chassis.description: It's commonly known that machines have 'personality'. Add me to a regular ol' machine stand and forever imbue it with 'blockmaker' personality.

blockmaker_engine.name: Machine Engine
blockmaker_engine.description: Install this engine, and your machine is one step closer to purring like the quintessential classic, thrumming like the deeply resonant inner chambers of Zille's enormous throat.

blockmaker_plates.name: Blockmaker Plates
blockmaker_plates.description: Made from only the finest Molybdenum this side of Shimla Mirch. Once installed, the Blockmaker is complete.

board.name: Board
board.description: A solid wooden board, reminiscent of some kind of ginormous Plank – unsurprisingly, since that's what it's made of.

boot.name: Boot
boot.description: This sure looks like a boot, eh?

bread.name: Bread
bread.description: Simple baked bread. Hearty and filling.

brick.name: Brick
brick.description: A solid, oblong rectangular mass, useful to those who build.

broccoli.name: Broccoli
broccoli.description: One floret of unabashedly healthy broccoli.

broken_sign.name: Bug Acknowledgment
broken_sign.description: We are aware of the issue and are working on it!

bubble.name: Bubble
bubble.description: A shimmering bubble harvested from a Bubble Tree.

bubble_and_squeak.name: Bubble and Squeak
bubble_and_squeak.description: Meaty, carroty, potato-y mush, fried to perfection.

bubble_tuner.name: Bubble Tuner
bubble_tuner.description: A Bubble Tuner somehow converts plain bubbles into not-plain bubbles. How? Nobody knows.

bubble_wand.name: Bubble Wand
bubble_wand.description: A wand for blowing iridescent bubbles.

bulb.name: Bulb
bulb.description: The glowy bit of a lamp. Without this, a lamp is just a stick with a silly hat on.

bunch_of_grapes.name: Bunch of Grapes
bunch_of_grapes.description: A heaping handful of red grapes.

bunch_of_grapes_hell.name: Bunch of Hellish Grapes
bunch_of_grapes_hell.description: A deceptively innocuous-looking bunch of hellish grapes.

butler_box.name: Special One-Time Delivery
butler_box.description: Special delivery, just for you! Whatever could it be?

butterfly_butter.name: Butterfly Butter
butterfly_butter.description: Butterfly butter might seem like an odd thing, but it is used in many delicious cooking recipes.

butterfly_egg.name: Butterfly Egg
butterfly_egg.description: An egg seasoned with the specific mix of lepidopteral herbs and spices to hatch a Caterpillar.

butterfly_lotion.name: Butterfly Lotion
butterfly_lotion.description: A tiny tube of butterfly lotion, without which a butterfly cannot be massaged. Remember: butterflies chafe easily, but they still like it pretty hard.

butterfly_milker.name: Butterfly Milker
butterfly_milker.description: A mechanized butterfly milker. It will gently milk your butterflies in your absence. Good for busy farmers or those with chapped hands.

butterfly_net.name: Butterfly Net
butterfly_net.description: It sure makes catching Butterflies easier, but at what cost?

cabbage.name: Cabbage
cabbage.description: A head of tender, delicately pungent cabbage.

camera.name: Camera
camera.description: A useful handheld gizmo with a lens, a button, and lots of complex intricate technical gubbins on the inside. Depending on who you ask, cameras are either for taking pretty pictures, or for stealing the soul of whoever they point at. This one is mainly for pictures though. You'd need a much bigger memory card for souls.

camphor.name: Camphor
camphor.description: A small pile of bitter camphor.

card_carrying_qualification.name: Card-Carrying Qualification
card_carrying_qualification.description: This is the most basic, entry-level, bureaucratic item of all. Being qualified to carry cards is a prerequisite to becoming the sort of person who can have Papers at all, let alone Papers which are in order.

cardamom.name: Cardamom
cardamom.description: A generous pinch of zesty cardamom. A little goes a long way.

carobish_treats.name: Raw Bio-Organic Carob-ish Treat
carobish_treats.description: A chewy, crunchy, apparently ethically-sourced, gritty 'candy-alternative' with a whiff of winter. Leaves a unique chocolatey aftertang you can't quite put your finger on.

cauldron.name: Cauldron
cauldron.description: A charmed pot for potioneers of all levels. This thick, heavy, magic-imbued cauldron is perfect for potions, from novice concoctions to expert unctions.

cedar_plank_salmon.name: Cedar Plank Salmon
cedar_plank_salmon.description: A wild salmon cooked on a wild cedar plank.

cheese.name: Cheese
cheese.description: A nice wedge of holey cheese.

cheese_stinky.name: Stinky Cheese
cheese_stinky.description: A lump of cheese that's been left to stand around for a while. It's what the French call 'ripe'. You could age it longer, but glory only knows what the French would call it then.

cheese_very_stinky.name: Very Stinky Cheese
cheese_very_stinky.description: Smelling like a manatee bathed in celery juice and old surgical stockings, goodness knows what would happen if you aged this connoisseurial cheese any longer. You could give it a go, though…

cheese_very_very_stinky.name: Very, Very Stinky Cheese
cheese_very_very_stinky.description: This is the very, very stinkiest cheese you've ever seen, or it would be if you could see through the thick veil of tears it's inducing. You can't make this any stinkier. It wouldn't be fair to everyone else.

cheezy_sauce.name: Cheezy Sauce
cheezy_sauce.description: A crock full of pleasingly, teasingly cheezy sauce.

cherry.name: Cherry
cherry.description: A basic cherry, freshly picked from a Fruit Tree.

cherry_pie.name: Cherry Pie
cherry_pie.description: A delicious pie baked with fresh cherries.

chick.name: Chick
chick.description: A wide-eyed chick, fresh from the egg. It needs grain in order to reach full chickenhood.

chicken_egg.name: Chicken Egg
chicken_egg.description: An egg treated with special poultry seasoning to hatch a Chick.

chillybusting_chili.name: Chilly-Busting Chili
chillybusting_chili.description: A tureen of fiery chilly-busting chili.

choice_crudites.name: Choice Crudites
choice_crudites.description: A platter of the very finest crudites.

cinnamon.name: Cinnamon
cinnamon.description: About a spoonful of versatile cinnamon.

class_axe.name: Class Axe
class_axe.description: Vibrating with raw power far beyond the realm of the basic Hatchet, the Class Axe is perfect for wielding by anyone who really loves to whack their wood.

cloud_11_smoothie.name: Cloud 11 Smoothie
cloud_11_smoothie.description: A Cloud 11 smoothie. Pretty much the best smoothie ever invented in the history of smoothies.

cloudberry_daiquiri.name: Cloudberry Daiquiri
cloudberry_daiquiri.description: A nourishing cloudberry daiquiri. It goes down smooth.

cloudberry_jam.name: Cloudberry Jam
cloudberry_jam.description: A jar of tart cloudberry jam.

club_house_picture.name: Curious Painting
club_house_picture.description: A curious painting found in the club house. There's something special about the feel of it.

cocktail_shaker.name: Cocktail Shaker
cocktail_shaker.description: A shiny, chrome-plated cocktail shaker for making special drinks.

coffee.name: Coffee
coffee.description: A steaming mug of strong, life-affirming coffee.

collectors_edition_2010_glitchmas_yeti.name: Collectors' Edition 2010 Glitchmas Yeti
collectors_edition_2010_glitchmas_yeti.description: A rare collector's edition stuffed Yeti for the wonderous, goodly, splendid Glitchen who helped test the world in 2010. This item is Limited Edition: if you sell it, give it away, donate it or otherwise lose it, you will not be able to get it back.

common_crudites.name: Common Crudites
common_crudites.description: Everyday crudites served in an easy-to-carry cup.

construction_tool.name: Construction Tool
construction_tool.description: One handy pocketsized gadget for all your furniture construction needs. Comes complete with four pokey whojamaflilps, one specializing in tweaking the wotsit, one in cranking the doodah, and the other two in levering the begiantus out of any sticky-outty bits. A true professional's tool.

contraband.name: Contraband
contraband.description: This is a surprisingly light package of *something* given to you by a smuggler who might ask you to take it from one place to another for reasons you cannot fully grasp. Still, a job's a job, right? What business is it of yours what's in the package. It DOES smell familiar, though…

copper.name: Copper Ingot
copper.description: Pliable yet strong, malleably magnificent, copper is a much-prized material for building. A glitch bearing good, strong copper will always be welcome on a building project.

corn.name: Corn
corn.description: Many kernels of sweet corn, conveniently located on a sturdy cob.

corn_off_the_cob.name: Corn-off-the-Cob
corn_off_the_cob.description: All the goodness of corn-on-the-cob, without the cob.

corn_syrup_squares.name: Hi-Sucrose Corn Syrup Square
corn_syrup_squares.description: Densely-packed brick of pure brain-melting sweetness, proving that sometimes the sum of many food items is either something that is only fit for use as building material in a fairy unicorn happy-palace, or a candy.

cornucopia_crops.name: Cornucopia of Crops
cornucopia_crops.description: A cornucopicone containing the ripe essence of every Crop imaginable, it smells like wet earth and evangelical vegophila. Too packed with fibre to actually eat, this can be transformed (by someone with appropriate Piety and enough influence with Mab) into a Blessed Cornucopia of Crops.

cornucopia_herbs.name: Cornucopia of Herbs
cornucopia_herbs.description: The crux of every herb in Ur, gathered together into richly scented cornucopicone. On its own, as useless as a large pot of savoury pot pourri, but with the help of someone with enough Piety and influence with Mab, it can be transformed into a much more magical Blessed Cornucopia of Herbs.

cosmox.name: Cosmox
cosmox.description: A compound made out of red, blue and shiny.

crabato_juice.name: Crabato Juice
crabato_juice.description: Mmm... all the goodness of crab juice, plus tomatoes!

crabphones.name: CrabPod Headphones
crabphones.description: These appear to be the kind of headphones only a crab could love. The sound quality is fabulous, but they really pinch your earlobes.

creamy_catsup.name: Creamy Catsup
creamy_catsup.description: One gallon of the creamiest catsup in all the land.

crusher.name: Crusher
crusher.description: Beware of the Crusher. For it will, as the name implies, crush.

crying_gas.name: Crying Gas
crying_gas.description: A flask of crying gas, saltily infused with real tears.

crystallizer.name: Crystalmalizing Chamber
crystallizer.description: With this tool, every armchair alchemist can be the master of their own future. Or at least make some Plain Crystals. Who knew Barnacle Talc and essence of Firefly would combine to make such fine new age bling?

cubimal_package.name: Cubimal Series 1 Box
cubimal_package.description: Contains one (1) Series 1 Cubimal. Open it up to find out which one is inside. Collect them all!

cubimal_package_2.name: Cubimal Series 2 Box
cubimal_package_2.description: Contains one (1) Series 2 Cubimal. Open it up to find out which one is inside. Collect 'em all!

cumin.name: Cumin
cumin.description: A heap of pleasantly aromatic cumin.

cup_of_water.name: Cup of Water
cup_of_water.description: This water is so plain, it has no Brownian motion, but Beigien motion instead. Drink at own risk.

curry.name: Curry
curry.description: A top-secret blend of curry powder.

death_to_veg.name: Death to Veg
death_to_veg.description: For those who wish a violent end to all harmless vegetables, and desire to feast upon their flesh.

desssert_rub.name: Desssert Rub
desssert_rub.description: Gritty, sandlike, and strangely sweet, this is both sandy like the desert, and sweet like dessert, and, as such, has the s's of both.

diabolic_acid.name: Diabolic Acid
diabolic_acid.description: A compound made out of red, green and blue.

dice_12sided.name: 12-sided Die
dice_12sided.description: This die has 12 sides. One for every Giant, plus a mystery guest.

dirt_pile.name: Dirt Pile
dirt_pile.description: A pile of dirt. Digging it with a shovel produces various dirty rewards, their quality depending on how deeply you appreciate soil, and how many friends are digging with you.

doll_ayn_rand.name: Ayn Rand Doll
doll_ayn_rand.description: Contradictions do not exist: but this doll does. Every time you cuddle your doll, you feel like it was the doll's idea, and you had no choice but to submit. Weird.

doll_nietzsche.name: Friedrich Nietzsche Doll
doll_nietzsche.description: He sure is talky, but this moustachioed little chap isn't very comforting to cuddle. But then how cuddly, exactly, were you expecting your My Little Nietzsche to be?

doll_wittgenstein.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein Doll
doll_wittgenstein.description: A depressed, closeted, singularly-focused Anglicized Austrian: student, schoolteacher, professor, maniac ... genius?

door_key_1.name: Yellow-Purple Triangle Key
door_key_1.description: As purple as it is yellow, this tri-pointed key has never met a door it didn't like.

door_key_10.name: Blue-Yellow Circle Key
door_key_10.description: POW! Right in the keyhole! That's what this blue and yellow circular key would say if it could speak. Which it can't.

door_key_11.name: Purple-Red Square Key
door_key_11.description: Purple, red and square, this key is a door's best friend. A friend with benefits. Door-opening benefits.

door_key_12.name: Green-Orange Hexagon Key
door_key_12.description: This key's greenness and orangeosity is matched only by its hexagonaliciousness.

door_key_2.name: Green-White Circle Key
door_key_2.description: Green and white and shaped like your melon, this little key will take you to the other side. Well, the other side of a door, anyway.

door_key_3.name: Orange-Blue Square Key
door_key_3.description: Does this orange and blue square key smell funny to you? This probably won't affect its door-opening abilities. Though you might want to wash your hands later.

door_key_4.name: Teal-Red Hexagon Key
door_key_4.description: Hexagonally shaped keys have more fun. Especially the teal and red ones, which seem to glide into keyholes with more eagerness than one would expect from an average key.

door_key_5.name: Red-Green Triangle Key
door_key_5.description: There's nothing a red-green triangle key loves more than a good door-opening. Nothing.

door_key_6.name: Purple-Orange Circle Key
door_key_6.description: Purple, orange and circular, this key has everything you could want in a key. Especially since it actually opens doors.

door_key_7.name: Yellow-Teal Square Key
door_key_7.description: Yellow, teal and square are good qualities in a key with door-opening aspirations.

door_key_8.name: Blue-White Hexagon Key
door_key_8.description: It's been said that hexagonal keys have more fun. Usually this is the case, except when said key is blue and white. These are serious keys for serious door openings.

door_key_9.name: Teal-White Triangle Key
door_key_9.description: Some say teal and white keys are nothing but trouble. They've never tried one of the triangular variety.

drink_ticket.name: Diabolical Drink Ticket
drink_ticket.description: Printed in infernal inks on fire-resistant paper, a Diabolical Drink Ticket can be given to Hell's Bartender in exchange for the potent Wine of the Dead—a fermented beverage which revitalizes the devitalized. No one knows what the Bartender does with the tickets, but rumour has it that in sufficient numbers they can be redeemed for either an all-expenses-paid vacation to Ix or the apocalypse.

dullite.name: Chunk of Dullite
dullite.description: A hefty chunk of dullite. What it lacks in sparkliness it makes up for in, um, dullness.

dust_trap.name: Dust Trap
dust_trap.description: Found in Baqala, intrepid explorers lucky enough to get caught in the snap of a dust trap are rewarded for their exploration with gifts and ancient remnants, which will allow more time for intrepid exploration.

dusty_stick.name: Dusty Stick
dusty_stick.description: A special gift to commemorate the Great Item Loss of early 2012. Build up the power of your stick throughout the day. When ready, release it in order to spread happiness to all those around you.

earth.name: Lump of Earth
earth.description: Many have tried to sum up the wonder of Earth, with superlatives like "Earthy", "Lumpy" and "Eart... oh, that's been said? Brown, then." But it's just a lump of earth. For building with.

egg_plain.name: Egg
egg_plain.description: A plain, unseasoned egg, newly harvested from an Egg Plant. It can be used for all kinds of cooking, or it can be hatched into different animals.

egg_seasoner.name: Egg Seasoner
egg_seasoner.description: An Egg Seasoner is an important tool for seasoning and incubating eggs that hatch into all kinds of exciting animals.

egghunt_egg_1.name: Certified Organic Chocolate Egg
egghunt_egg_1.description: An 'oh-so-delicious' and delightfully decorated chocolate egg. Inarguably tasty, this treat is not affiliated with any known holiday or group, religious or otherwise.

egghunt_egg_2.name: Bugsbury Creme Egg
egghunt_egg_2.description: An 'oh-so-delicious' and delightfully decorated chocolate egg. Inarguably tasty, this treat is not affiliated with any known holiday or group, religious or otherwise.

egghunt_egg_3.name: Pysanka
egghunt_egg_3.description: An 'oh-so-delicious' and delightfully decorated chocolate egg. Inarguably tasty, this treat is not affiliated with any known holiday or group, religious or otherwise.

egghunt_egg_4.name: Secular Holiday Egg
egghunt_egg_4.description: An 'oh-so-delicious' and delightfully decorated chocolate egg. Inarguably tasty, this treat is not affiliated with any known holiday or group, religious or otherwise.

egghunt_egg_5.name: Golden Egg
egghunt_egg_5.description: An 'oh-so-delicious' and delightfully decorated chocolate egg. Inarguably tasty, this treat is not affiliated with any known holiday or group, religious or otherwise.

eggy_scramble.name: Eggy Scramble
eggy_scramble.description: A messily scrumptious pile of eggs.

element_blue.name: Blue Element
element_blue.description: An element is the basic building block of all stuff. This one is blue.

element_green.name: Green Element
element_green.description: An element is the basic building block of all stuff. This one is green.

element_red.name: Red Element
element_red.description: An element is the basic building block of all stuff. This one is red.

element_shiny.name: Shiny Element
element_shiny.description: An element is the basic building block of all stuff. This one is shiny.

emblem_alph.name: Emblem of Alph
emblem_alph.description: As valuable as it is shiny, this Emblem of Alph enables special skills and abilities.

emblem_cosma.name: Emblem of Cosma
emblem_cosma.description: More than just a highly cherishable gewgaw, this glistening Emblem of Cosma can confer special powers upon its holder.

emblem_friendly.name: Emblem of Friendly
emblem_friendly.description: Radiantly important and importantly radiant, this Emblem of Friendly can unlock special talents.

emblem_grendaline.name: Emblem of Grendaline
emblem_grendaline.description: This lustrous Emblem of Grendaline bestows some fairly impressive abilities upon its lucky owner.

emblem_humbaba.name: Emblem of Humbaba
emblem_humbaba.description: Behold a dazzling Emblem of Humbaba. Keep it safe, and someday it may entitle you to wield special powers and whatnot.

emblem_lem.name: Emblem of Lem
emblem_lem.description: Almost blinding in its glory, this Emblem of Lem may allow you to do some seriously fancy things.

emblem_mab.name: Emblem of Mab
emblem_mab.description: A highly coveted, moderately burnished Emblem of Mab. If you're both smart and lucky, it may confer special skills upon you.

emblem_pot.name: Emblem of Pot
emblem_pot.description: Gleaming as if lit from within, this Emblem of Pot looks like the kind of thing you should hold on to. It may just come in handy down the road, hm?

emblem_spriggan.name: Emblem of Spriggan
emblem_spriggan.description: This highly polished Emblem of Spriggan is aglow with a golden light. It's probably important, don't you think?

emblem_ti.name: Emblem of Tii
emblem_ti.description: This Emblem of Tii is more than just a flashy tchotchke to add to your flashy tchotchke collection. How much more? That's for you to find out.

emblem_zille.name: Emblem of Zille
emblem_zille.description: Containing a gleam as if from a sense of its own importance, this Zille Emblem is worth holding on to. Why? You'll see.

emotional_bear.name: Emotional Bear
emotional_bear.description: When you can't express how you're feeling, this furry companion will emote on your behalf. Particularly when equipped with Lips for kissing or a Moon for you-know-whatting…

essence_of_gandlevery.name: Essence of Gandlevery
essence_of_gandlevery.description: A potent distillation of Gandlevery, as rich in protein and good for energy as it is foul on the tongue and bad for mood.

essence_of_hairball.name: Essence of Hairball
essence_of_hairball.description: All the speed-giving power of the Hairball Flower, scraped from the individual hairball ball-hairs, and concentrated into a tincture.

essence_of_purple.name: Essence of Purple
essence_of_purple.description: You thought Purple was purple? This is seriously purple. Seriously: The purplest.

essence_of_rookswort.name: Essence of Rookswort
essence_of_rookswort.description: Distilled Rookswort harnesses the power of the ancients for ultra-absorbent protection during those heavy rook attacks.

essence_of_rubeweed.name: Essence of Rubeweed
essence_of_rubeweed.description: The purest scent of gullible luck, boiled down to the point where Rubes can smell it from many many planks away.

essence_of_silvertongue.name: Essence of Silvertongue
essence_of_silvertongue.description: The concentrated charm of Silvertongue, giving large returns on upcoming ventures.

essence_of_yellow_crumb.name: Essence of Yellow Crumb
essence_of_yellow_crumb.description: Purest concentration, concentrated, for those who need a more expansive brain-span.

exotic_fruit_salad.name: Exotic Fruit Salad
exotic_fruit_salad.description: An enticing array of exotic fruits.

exotic_juice.name: Exotic Juice
exotic_juice.description: A goblet of mysteriously yummy exotic juice.

expensive_grilled_cheese.name: Expensive Grilled Cheese
expensive_grilled_cheese.description: Expensive cheese expensively grilled on expensive bread.

extremely_hallowed_shrine_powder.name: Extremely Hallowed Shrine Powder
extremely_hallowed_shrine_powder.description: A jar of extremely hallowed shrine powder. It lets the giants know that, when it comes to currying favor, you really mean business.

face_smelter.name: Face Smelter
face_smelter.description: A powerful brew with a scent acrid enough to perm your nostril hairs.

faded_heart.name: Faded Heart
faded_heart.description: This ghostly, Faded Heart is all that remains of a forgotten tragedy.

fairly_hallowed_shrine_powder.name: Fairly Hallowed Shrine Powder
fairly_hallowed_shrine_powder.description: Curry a moderate amount of favor with the giants with a jar of fairly hallowed shrine powder.

fancy_pick.name: Fancy Pick
fancy_pick.description: A super-deluxe fancy pick for fancy miners. Okay, maybe it doesn't look that fancy on the outside. It's the inside that counts.

fertilidust.name: Fertilidust
fertilidust.description: A jar of chokingly musty fertilidust, effective in promoting the health of trees.

fertilidust_lite.name: Fertilidust Lite
fertilidust_lite.description: A jar of somewhat musty fertilidust lite, effective in improving the health of individual trees.

fiber.name: Fiber
fiber.description: A patch of downy hair brushed from the tail of a fox, perfect for spinning into thread or stuffing into furniture for padding. Also might be perfect for gluing to your face as a moustache, but due to its origin (the underside of a fox tail), no one does.

firefly_jar.name: Firefly Jar
firefly_jar.description: This is no ordinary jar. Well, it IS, but once filled with fireflies (seven maximum: objects in space need room to fly) just looking at it gives you a great sense of serenity.

firefly_whistle.name: Firefly Whistle
firefly_whistle.description: A Firefly Whistle, perfect for whistling at fireflies. I wonder if that's how it got its name.

flummery.name: Flummery
flummery.description: Few things in this life are as tasty as a masterfully prepared flummery. "Just like Mom used to make!" you might say. If you had a mom, that is.

focusing_orb.name: Focusing Orb
